PARAM.description: errorMessageTemplate must not be null, and must contain %s placeholders
PARAM.name: errorMessageTemplate
PARAM.content: a template for the exception message should the check fail. The message is formed by replacing each %s placeholder in the template with an argument.

PARAM.description: errorMessageArgs must not be null, and must contain exactly one %s placeholder
PARAM.name: errorMessageArgs
PARAM.content: the arguments to be substituted into the message template. Arguments are converted to strings using String#valueOf(Object).

PARAM.name: errorMessageTemplate
PARAM.content:
a template for the exception message should the
    check fail. The message is formed by replacing each {@code %s}
    placeholder in the template with an argument. These are matched by
    position - the first {@code %s} gets {@code errorMessageArgs[0]}, etc.
    Unmatched arguments will be appended to the formatted message in square
    braces. Unmatched placeholders will be left as-is.
PARAM.description:
a template for the exception message should the
    check fail. The message is formed by replacing each {@code %s}
    placeholder in the template with an argument. These are matched by
    position - the first {@code %s} gets {@code errorMessageArgs[0]}, etc.
    Unmatched arguments will be appended to the formatted message in square
    braces. Unmatched placeholders will be left as-is.

PARAM.name: errorMessageArgs
PARAM.content:
the arguments to be substituted into the message
    template. Arguments are converted to strings using
    {@link String#valueOf(Object)}.
PARAM.description:
the arguments to be substituted into the message
    template. Arguments are converted to strings using
    {@link String#valueOf(Object)}.

RETURN.content: {@code reference}, guaranteed to be non-null, for convenience
